ab: te’o peela tuocosi na suu?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: naho, te’o na’no kaebʉ.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: te’o peela tuocosi na suu?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: o’ate, o’amos’a man’i na os’o co’cohivi.
en: B:Don't, I don't know much about it.

ab: teko meemelʉ ho atavei no cohzona? teko tiova honci’u usa ho aiti na suu?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: naho, ta’u yonta emoo ho totea na suu.
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: tako meemelʉ ho hucma? nte’o mici tuucocosʉ suu no h’oehanva ta cou a’toana.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於鄒族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I'd like to ask you about the legendary story of the Tsou people.

ab: ta’u uhne fuengu ho hucma, o’a ta’us’a yonta emoo.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: o’u totalʉi ho leako na’no bocbochio tola hia h’ocoecona ta cou ne noan’o, mi’o mici tuucocosʉ suu.
ch: A:我聽說您對鄒族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I've heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Tsou people, so I'd like to ask you a few questions.

ab: o’a mos’a man’i na os’o co’cohivi, at’inghi ta’u pnanfi ho eʉsvʉsvʉta.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: te’o tuocosʉn’a, te’o peela hu’ongki? tenala ko’ko peela ʉmnʉ ci tpotposi.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: naho, tee eno kuzo.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: miko cu topzoha ta tonsoha maitan’e?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: ma mi’ocu tomsiyoha uveo.
en: B: I'm 98 years old.

ab: ci mikon’a na’no mifoinana.
en: A: You look so young.

ab: ma o’a, ma mi’ocu mameoi.
en: B: No, I'm not! I'm old.

ab: mikos’a i’mi nenu?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: mi’o i’mi ne pnguu.
en: B: I'm from Laikichi.

ab: zou maezo cou a’toana na vcongʉsu?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是鄒族嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a Chow?

ab: o’asa’a, ’a sbukunu.
en: B: No, she's a Bunun.

ab: mo pio ci h’oe’ea na lako peelʉi?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: la’u meelʉ bua cou ho bua sbukunu.
ch: B: 我會說鄒語和布農語。
en: B: I speak the Tsou and Bunun languages.

ab: na’no kaebʉ ho os’o tuucocosi suu maitan’e, tekon’a eʉsvʉta, nala peesi hola e’ohʉ ea fou zou?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: ’a tee eno mo’u la na’nosino uhne fuengu ho ea fou ne mo’un’a foinana.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: ’aveoveoyʉ , a’ʉmtʉ na’no ʉmnʉ ’e iko pa’cohivneni maitan’e.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: i’e os’o eʉsvʉsvʉta maitan’e mo a’ʉmtu emzo, ohela h’oehamva to niala acngicngihi maameoi.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: tec’o ec’ʉha tan’e na’no ’aveoveoyʉ ta iko eʉsvʉsvʉta.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: upena tatola i’vaho yupeahngʉyʉ.
en: B: No problem, we'll talk again.

ab: te’ocu mongoi, tata’za honcila i’vaho tmalʉ no man’i ci ehohamvasu.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到你更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more of your story next time.

ab: zou, nahoeno aupopoha’o.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
